title: centaurea alba subsp. ciliata
intro: Centaurea alba subsp. ciliata, also known as White Knapweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of White Knapweed can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
